findings.evidence: `FiniteCofree.lean:46-57` uses factor finiteness only to call `FGComoduleCat.of` on `M ⊗[R] C`; `Finite.lean:136-137` shows `of` needs exactly `[Module.Finite R M]` for the bundled underlying module.
findings.fix: Make the primary `cofree` assume `[Module.Finite R (M ⊗[R] C)]`, and make `cofreeMap` assume finite generation of the source and target tensor products; users with finite `M`, `N`, and `C` will still get these by `Module.Finite.tensorProduct`.
findings.issue: `FGComoduleCat.cofree` and `cofreeMap` globally require `[Module.Finite R C]` and finite coefficient modules, but the object only needs `Module.Finite R (M ⊗[R] C)`; this excludes valid finite cofree objects such as zero coefficients over a non-finitely generated coalgebra.

summary: The finite cofree construction is stated with stronger finiteness hypotheses than the finite subcategory actually requires.
